_scoring_notes.kinetic_formula: 40% intercept rate (scaled 90-100% → 0-100pts) + 20% absolute debris penalty (100 - debris×8) + 20% day-over-day trend (100 - delta×10, capped 0-100) + 20% 7-day avg trend (100 - delta×10, capped 0-100)
_scoring_notes.social_formula: Base 100. Deductions: -5 per NCEMA alert in last 24h, -10 US embassy Level 3 advisory, -10 per full embassy departure advisory, -5 per airport partial disruption, -10 per airport closure

social_inputs.ukFCDOAdvisory: Advise against all but essential travel — no change
social_inputs.dxbStatus: Operational with weather delays (adverse weather Mar 23-27)

dailyBriefing: March 23 saw UAE air defences achieve their best single-day performance since the conflict began, intercepting all 7 ballistic missiles and 16 drones launched from Iran (100% intercept rate). Only 1 debris incident was confirmed: an Indian national sustained minor injuries in Al Shawamekh (Abu Dhabi industrial area). No Dubai neighbourhood debris was reported. Dubai Police issued a weather advisory clarifying that thunder sounds should not be confused with interceptions. DXB and AUH remain operational with weather-related delays (adverse conditions expected Mar 23-27); IndiGo has cancelled Dubai flights through April 30. Diplomatically, Trump announced a 5-day pause on strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure citing 'productive talks', though Iran's foreign ministry denied any negotiations. Iran's Defence Council threatened to mine the entire Persian Gulf if a land invasion occurs, and published target maps of Gulf power plants including the UAE nuclear plant. The US Embassy UAE maintained its Level 3 'Reconsider Travel' advisory. UAE government stance remains normal life with alerts followed as issued.

schoolClosures.label: Distance Learning Only
schoolClosures.guidance: Schools remain closed for in-person attendance. Universities and colleges have moved to online classes as a precautionary measure. Monitor KHDA for reopening dates.

supermarketStock.label: Adequate — Air Freight Supplement
supermarketStock.guidance: Major chains (Carrefour, Lulu, Spinneys) remain stocked. Air freight supplements continue to offset Hormuz shipping disruptions. Some imported goods showing price increases.

fuelAvailability.label: Normal Supply
fuelAvailability.guidance: ENOC and ADNOC stations fully operational across Dubai. No queues or shortages reported. Oil prices remain elevated globally but no local supply impact.
